# csRevit Full Core Blueprint, Documentation, and Code Boilerplates

This file contains the complete operational specification, installation procedures, and full-fidelity code boilerplates for the csRevit Microkernel ecosystem. It is optimized for direct consumption by LLMs and AI coding assistants.

---

## 1. System Overview & Core Architecture

csRevit is a commercial, enterprise-grade native C# Sync Engine and Microkernel framework for Autodesk Revit that completely decouples User Interface generation from compiled code. It serves as a high-performance alternative to legacy scripting solutions, allowing firms to scale internal automation seamlessly.

### Core Architectural Mechanics:
- **Zero-Restart UI Generation**: Ribbons, tabs, panels, and items are dynamically generated on the fly based on Windows folder hierarchies and explicit naming suffixes (e.g., `.tab`, `.panel`, `.pushbutton`). UI changes reflect mid-session upon triggering a sync without restarting active Revit user sessions.
- **Multi-Version Concurrency**: Designed to run cleanly across different runtime systems natively (.NET Framework 4.8 for Revit 2019-2024 vs. .NET 8.0 for Revit 2025-2027+) without causing file lock collisions or assembly environment domain crashes.
- **The Loader Engine**: A native bootstrapper client placed locally inside `%AppData%\Autodesk\ApplicationPlugins\csRevit.bundle` initializes on Revit startup, parses designated directories, and builds out ribbon mappings dynamically.
- **Telemetry & Logs**: Automatically updates a centralized, thread-safe telemetry queue logging execution metrics, active users, and framework health metrics to a localized `Telemetry.csv` file on the organization's master drive.

---

## 2. Global Installation & Deployment Guide

### System Prerequisites
- **Autodesk Revit**: Versions 2019 through 2026 natively installed.
- **Operating System**: Microsoft Windows 10 or Windows 11 (x64 platform target).
- **Network Paths**: Active read/write credentials to a shared corporate central deployment target drive or folder structure (e.g., `Z:\BIM\csRevit_Master`).
- **Licensing Core**: Active commercial subscription key or authorized 15-day free trial key provisioned securely via the official merchant webstore (https://appstore.svecs.in).

### Bootstrap Setup Sequence
1. Secure the executable deployment pack (`csRevit_Installer.exe`) from your management account portal.
2. Run the deployment pack. The bootstrapper installer drops runtime client binaries into the user application data directory:
   `C:\Users\%USERNAME%\AppData\Roaming\Autodesk\ApplicationPlugins\csRevit.bundle`
3. Launch Autodesk Revit. Paste either the 15-Day Free Trial Key or Commercial License Key into the secure validation panel.
4. Navigate to the newly generated `csRevit Core` tab on the ribbon, click **Global Settings** (Gear Icon), and link the **Master Network Path** field directly to the shared network storage folder (e.g., `Z:\BIM\csRevit_Master`).
5. Select a discipline classification filter (e.g., `Architecture`, `Structure`, or `All`) to govern structural visibility filters.
6. Click **Sync Tools** to instantly build out the custom tools ribbon mid-session.
7. *IT Admin Note:* For silent corporate deployment across large workstation setups via Intune/SCCM, command line installation arguments are managed via private enterprise channels.

### Local Sandbox Development Workflow
To safely prototype and compile new commands without altering production corporate environments, developers utilize a localized testing sandbox:
1. Local staging testing directory: `C:\Users\%USERNAME%\AppData\Roaming\csRevit\DEV`
2. Any structured directory folder configurations placed in this workspace generate a dedicated `🛠️ DEV LAB` test tab dynamically inside Revit.
3. Developers configure their project configurations (`.csproj`) to deploy compiled assets instantly to this folder workspace, then select **Reload UI** on the core tab to observe execution changes instantly.
4. To move verified code live, developers use the staging pipeline to securely relocate the folder schemas directly to the Master Network Drive production folder.

---

## 3. Ribbon Generation, Manifest Layouts & Folder Rules

The execution engine reads file directory extensions and names to formulate UI elements without hardcoded C# formatting.

### Directory Naming Rules:
- **Prefix Sorting**: Numbers followed by an underscore (e.g., `01_`) govern layout positions from left-to-right or top-to-bottom. The parsing engine automatically strips these prefixes from the visual ribbon element. For example, `01_Modify.panel` renders cleanly as **Modify**.
- **Suffix Mapping**:
  - `.tab` — Establishes a new primary ribbon category tab.
  - `.panel` — Registers a functional grouping layout container.
  - `.pushbutton` — Instantiates a single command item button execution loop.
  - `.pulldown` — Maps a vertical drop-down list grouping container.
  - `.splitbutton` — Creates an automated toggle split-button interface.
  - `.stack` — Vertically compresses up to three buttons together to conserve screen layout.

### Folder Directory Payload Architecture:
An active command directory (e.g., `Export PDF.pushbutton`) must contain the following structural file components to evaluate correctly:
- `R24L/` (Folder) — Hosts compiled binaries target-built for .NET Framework 4.8 (Revit 2024 and older).
- `R25H/` (Folder) — Hosts compiled binaries target-built for .NET 8.0 (Revit 2025 and newer).
- `icon.png` — Visual graphic file (32 x 32 pixels). The loader automatically scales this down to 16 x 16 if the tool resides inside a vertical `.stack` file setup.
- `help.txt` *(Optional)* — String literal data that automatically maps to the native F1 tool tooltip layout.
- `demo.mp4` *(Optional)* — Short video asset that streams inside the tooltip popup menu on user mouse hover events.
- `class.txt` *(Optional)* — Standard text string specifying the exact target entry-point C# Class name when grouping multiple executable tools inside a shared, singular compiled assembly DLL file.
- `config.json` *(Required for Comboboxes)* — Array map file specifying option choices populated inside the combo box container dropdown view.

---

## 4. Software Development Kit (SDK) & Engineering Guidelines

All custom extensions, plug-in additions, and script payloads generated for use inside the framework must explicitly comply with the following 6 Golden Rules of csRevit Engineering:

### The 6 Golden Rules of csRevit Engineering:
1. **Interface Binding**: Direct UI components must cleanly inherit from `Autodesk.Revit.UI.IExternalCommand`. Background automated engines or pane interfaces must implement `Autodesk.Revit.UI.IExternalApplication`.
2. **Transaction Scoping**: Every external command implementation class must be declared with an explicit database runtime behavior marker attribute: `[Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]`.
3. **UI Display Attributes**: Use `[DisplayName("Button Name")]` and `[Description("Tooltip Info")]` tags directly above class signatures to control global search matching configurations and display formatting.
4. **Platform Multi-Targeting**: Multi-target your project environments to cleanly emit runtime assemblies into both `.NET Framework 4.8` (`R24L`) configurations and `.NET 8.0` (`R25H`) targets.
5. **Assembly Linking Reference**: Link your assemblies against `csRevit.API.dll`. The base bootstrapper engine provides this library layout in memory on initialization.
6. **Reference Collision Preventions**: For every reference to native Revit binary dependencies (`RevitAPI.dll`, `RevitAPIUI.dll`) and our shared library `csRevit.API.dll`, the developer MUST absolutely toggle the Visual Studio property **`Copy Local = False`** (or `<Private>False</Private>` inside the XML configurations). Failing to implement this will result in fatal reference collision exceptions in the application domain memory footprint.

### WPF Interaction Paradigms:
- **Modal Dialogs**: Blocks interactions with the Revit workspace window frame layout until exited. Initiated from `.pushbutton` directories via `myWindow.ShowDialog();` within safe execution loops.
- **Modeless Palettes**: Keeps the user UI frames floating while allowing direct document element interactions. Loses immediate API context loop control. Developers must use Revit's native `IExternalEventHandler` and `ExternalEvent` mechanics to securely line up write transactions back onto the primary execution UI main thread.
- **Dockable Panes**: Snaps deep structural layouts alongside native Revit properties matrices. These cannot initialize on simple command clicks and must be built as a background service and registered via the `Services/` workspace pipeline on the boot initialization step.

---

## 5. Complete C# Code Boilerplate Templates


### A. Basic Pushbutton Script Layout (No Custom UI) with `HostEnvironment`

using System;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;
using Autodesk.Revit.Attributes;
using csRevit.API;

namespace csRevit_Tools
{
    [Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
    [DisplayName("Quick Format")]
    [Description("Formats the active view.")]
    public class QuickFormatCommand : IExternalCommand
    {
        public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData commandData, ref string message, ElementSet elements)
        {
            // Acquire Document reference context hooks safely
            Document doc = commandData.Application.ActiveUIDocument.Document;

            try
            {
                // Access core paths cleanly via the actual HostEnvironment API
                string root = HostEnvironment.RootFolder;
                string devPath = HostEnvironment.DevFolder;

                // Insert custom document modifications inside transaction scopes here
                using (Transaction t = new Transaction(doc, "Quick Format"))
                {
                    t.Start();
                    // Implementation goes here
                    t.Commit();
                }

                return Result.Succeeded;
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                message = ex.Message;
                return Result.Failed; // Automatically intercepts exceptions and routes errors to the csRevit Debug Console
            }
        }
    }
}


B. Standard Modal Dialog WPF Initialization & Theme Integration Template

using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;
using Autodesk.Revit.Attributes;
using csRevit.API;

namespace csRevit_Tools
{
    [Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
    public class LaunchModalCommand : IExternalCommand
    {
        public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData commandData, ref string message, ElementSet elements)
        {
            // 1. Initialize custom WPF window entity passing operational data context
            MyWpfWindow window = new MyWpfWindow(commandData.Application.ActiveUIDocument.Document);

            // 2. Integrate precise styling via ThemeHelper
            window.Background = ThemeHelper.GetBackground();
            window.Foreground = ThemeHelper.GetForeground();

            // 3. Attach the main Revit application window frame container as the parent handle to maintain correct window placement positioning
            var helper = new System.Windows.Interop.WindowInteropHelper(window);
            helper.Owner = System.Diagnostics.Process.GetCurrentProcess().MainWindowHandle;

            // 4. Display modally to lock operational threads safely until user resolution
            window.ShowDialog();

            return Result.Succeeded;
        }
    }
}


C. Modeless Dialog Palette & External Event Integration Template

using System;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;
using Autodesk.Revit.Attributes;

namespace csRevit_Tools
{
    // 1. THE ASYNC EVENT HANDLER: Safely processes external UI events on the primary native Revit engine thread layout
    public class MyModelessHandler : IExternalEventHandler
    {
        public void Execute(UIApplication app)
        {
            Document doc = app.ActiveUIDocument.Document;
            using (Transaction t = new Transaction(doc, "Modeless Modification"))
            {
                t.Start();
                // PLACE MODIFICATION LOGIC SECURELY HERE
                t.Commit();
            }
        }
        public string GetName() => "csRevit Modeless Handler";
    }

    // 2. THE COMMAND ENTRANCE TRIGGER: Instantiates the floating palette interface safely
    [Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
    public class LaunchModelessCommand : IExternalCommand
    {
        // Keep window instance static to maintain a singleton interface layout pattern 
        private static MyWpfWindow _modelessWindow = null; 

        public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData commandData, ref string message, ElementSet elements)
        {
            if (_modelessWindow != null && _modelessWindow.IsLoaded)
            {
                _modelessWindow.Focus(); // Re-focus interface if current workspace instance is already floating open
                return Result.Succeeded;
            }

            // Construct and bind our safe modeless execution bridge systems
            MyModelessHandler handler = new MyModelessHandler();
            ExternalEvent exEvent = ExternalEvent.Create(handler);

            // Inject the created event engine handler back directly into the custom WPF interface configuration
            _modelessWindow = new MyWpfWindow(exEvent);

            var helper = new System.Windows.Interop.WindowInteropHelper(_modelessWindow);
            helper.Owner = System.Diagnostics.Process.GetCurrentProcess().MainWindowHandle;

            // Display Modeless to allow continuous underlying document access functions
            _modelessWindow.Show();

            return Result.Succeeded;
        }
    }
}

D. Data-Driven State Toggle Button Template (.toggle)

using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;
using Autodesk.Revit.Attributes;
using csRevit.API; // Linked dependency for configuration tracking engines

namespace csRevit_Tools
{
    [Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
    [DisplayName("Auto Dimension")]
    public class AutoDimToggleCommand : IExternalCommand
    {
        public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData commandData, ref string message, ElementSet elements)
        {
            // Query the registry storage database to extract state assignments updated by the layout manager
            string currentState = SettingsManager.ReadSetting("ToggleStates", "Auto Dimension", "Off");

            if (currentState == "On")
            {
                TaskDialog.Show("Toggle", "Auto Dimensioning is now ENABLED.");
            }
            else
            {
                TaskDialog.Show("Toggle", "Auto Dimensioning is now DISABLED.");
            }

            return Result.Succeeded;
        }
    }
}


E. Data-Driven ComboBox Menu Selection Interface Template (.combobox)

using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;
using Autodesk.Revit.Attributes;
using csRevit.API; 

namespace csRevit_Tools
{
    [Transaction(TransactionMode.Manual)]
    [DisplayName("Select Discipline")]
    public class DisciplineComboBoxCommand : IExternalCommand
    {
        public Result Execute(ExternalCommandData commandData, ref string message, ElementSet elements)
        {
            // Intercept user selection metrics mapped dynamically into Registry cache spaces on click event actions
            string selectedValue = SettingsManager.ReadSetting("Select Discipline", "ComboBoxValue", "");

            TaskDialog.Show("Combo Box", $"You selected: {selectedValue}");

            // Execute programmatic actions relative to selected option parameters...

            return Result.Succeeded;
        }
    }
}

F. Idle/Background Application Event Tracking Template

using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Events;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;

namespace csRevit_Tools
{
    public class AutoAuditService : IExternalApplication
    {
        public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
        {
            // Attach functional listener endpoints safely to base application pipeline updates
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentOpened += OnDocumentOpened;
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentSaved += OnDocumentSaved;
            
            return Result.Succeeded;
        }

        public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
        {
            // Clear hook attachments strictly on exit steps to avert severe application memory leaks
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentOpened -= OnDocumentOpened;
            app.ControlledApplication.DocumentSaved -= OnDocumentSaved;
            
            return Result.Succeeded;
        }

        private void OnDocumentOpened(object sender, DocumentOpenedEventArgs e)
        {
            Document doc = e.Document;
            if (doc.IsFamilyDocument) return;

            // RUN SILENT DATA COMPLIANCE PROCESSING ASSIGNMENTS HERE
            // [csRevit-Architecture-Rule]: Background events must avoid native UI TaskDialog calls to protect silent batch runs.
        }

        private void OnDocumentSaved(object sender, DocumentSavedEventArgs e)
        {
            Document doc = e.Document;
            // Execute programmatic cloud synchronization logs or analytical metrics operations here
        }
    }
}


G. Hybrid SDK Connector Setup (Service Lifecycle + Interactive UI Trigger)

using Autodesk.Revit.DB;
using Autodesk.Revit.UI;
using csRevit.API;

namespace csRevit_MyHybridTool
{
    // PART 1: THE ENTERPRISE BACKGROUND DAEMON (Deployed into the global shared Services/ directory drive space)
    public class MyEnterpriseService : IExternalApplication, IcsRevitBackgroundService
    {
        // 1. Expose a public static thread-safe initialization handle so interactive UI commands can find this specific host service
        public static MyEnterpriseService Instance { get; private set; }

        // 2. Populate contract mandates for the IcsRevitBackgroundService engine interface from actual API properties
        public string ServiceName => "Enterprise Door Updater";
        public string Version => "1.0.0";
        public ServiceState CurrentState { get; private set; } = ServiceState.Offline;

        public Result OnStartup(UIControlledApplication app)
        {
            Instance = this;
            CurrentState = ServiceState.Idle; // Confirm ready status flags to underlying scheduler links
            return Result.Succeeded;
        }

        public Result OnShutdown(UIControlledApplication app)
        {
            CurrentState = ServiceState.Offline;
            return Result.Succeeded;
        }

        // 3. Execution pipeline endpoint invoked securely by clicking the foreground interface item
        public Result ExecuteTriggerFromUI(ExternalCommandData commandData, ref string message, ElementSet elements)
        {
            CurrentState = ServiceState.Processing; // Lock thread execution metrics to block dangerous execution collisions
            
            try
            {
                Document doc = commandData.Application.ActiveUIDocument.Document;
                TaskDialog.Show(ServiceName, "The UI Button successfully triggered the Background Service!");
                return Result.Succeeded;
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                message = ex.Message;
                CurrentState = ServiceState.Error;
                return Result.Failed;
            }
            finally
            {
                if (CurrentState != ServiceState.Error) CurrentState = ServiceState.Idle; // Unlock status tracks safely
            }
        }
    }
}

H. Automated MSBuild Project Compilation Manifest (.csproj)

XML<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFrameworks>net48;net8.0-windows</TargetFrameworks>
    <Platforms>x64</Platforms>
    <UseWPF>true</UseWPF>
    <GenerateAssemblyInfo>false</GenerateAssemblyInfo>
    <AppendTargetFrameworkToOutputPath>true</AppendTargetFrameworkToOutputPath>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net48'">
    <Reference Include="PresentationCore" />
    <Reference Include="PresentationFramework" />
    <Reference Include="WindowsBase" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xaml" />
    <Reference Include="RevitAPI">
      <HintPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2024\RevitAPI.dll</HintPath>
      <Private>False</Private>
    </Reference>
    <Reference Include="RevitAPIUI">
      <HintPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2024\RevitAPIUI.dll</HintPath>
      <Private>False</Private>
    </Reference>
    <Reference Include="csRevit.API">
      <HintPath>$(AppData)\Autodesk\ApplicationPlugins\csRevit.bundle\Contents\2024\csRevit.API.dll</HintPath>
      <Private>False</Private>
    </Reference>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0-windows'">
    <Reference Include="RevitAPI">
      <HintPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2025\RevitAPI.dll</HintPath>
      <Private>False</Private>
    </Reference>
    <Reference Include="RevitAPIUI">
      <HintPath>C:\Program Files\Autodesk\Revit 2025\RevitAPIUI.dll</HintPath>
      <Private>False</Private>
    </Reference>
    <Reference Include="csRevit.API">
      <HintPath>$(AppData)\Autodesk\ApplicationPlugins\csRevit.bundle\Contents\2025\csRevit.API.dll</HintPath>
      <Private>False</Private>
    </Reference>
  </ItemGroup>

  <Target Name="DeployToSandbox" AfterTargets="Build" Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' != ''">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <DevServicePath>$(AppData)\csRevit\DEV\Services\MyEnterpriseService</DevServicePath>
      <Btn1>$(AppData)\csRevit\DEV\02_Advanced.panel\Quick Select.splitbutton\Select All.pushbutton</Btn1>
      <Btn2>$(AppData)\csRevit\DEV\02_Advanced.panel\Quick Select.splitbutton\Select None.pushbutton</Btn2>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <Copy Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net48' And '$(DevServicePath)' != ''" SourceFiles="$(TargetPath)" DestinationFolder="$(DevServicePath)\R24L" />
    <Copy Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0-windows' And '$(DevServicePath)' != ''" SourceFiles="$(TargetPath)" DestinationFolder="$(DevServicePath)\R25H" />

    <Copy Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net48'" SourceFiles="$(TargetPath)" DestinationFolder="$(Btn1)\R24L" />
    <Copy Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0-windows'" SourceFiles="$(TargetPath)" DestinationFolder="$(Btn1)\R25H" />
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(Btn1)\class.txt" Lines="SelectAllCommand" Overwrite="true" />

    <Copy Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net48'" SourceFiles="$(TargetPath)" DestinationFolder="$(Btn2)\R24L" />
    <Copy Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0-windows'" SourceFiles="$(TargetPath)" DestinationFolder="$(Btn2)\R25H" />
    <WriteLinesToFile File="$(Btn2)\class.txt" Lines="SelectNoneCommand" Overwrite="true" />
  </Target>
</Project>

6. Commercial Terms & Closed-Source IntegrityIntellectual Property ProtectionThe core csRevit sync engine and compilation microkernel binaries are strictly closed-source, commercial property managed by SVECS BIM & AUTOMATION.Reverse engineering, decompiling compiled assets (.dll elements), or distributing the core installers via unauthorized mirroring infrastructure is explicit violation of the framework's End User License Agreement (EULA). All pre-sales or post-purchase technical inquiries must route through official corporate help channels.📩 Enterprise Support & Procurement Routing: support@svecs.in
